question: How does the proskyneo (worship) verb chain through Revelation 13-14 establish the worship binary as THE defining issue of the end-time crisis? Trace every occurrence of proskyneo in Rev 13-14: spontaneous (13:4), universal (13:8), coerced by the earth beast (13:12), death-enforced (13:15), then the Creator alternative (14:7) and the warning against beast worship (14:9,11). How does the Daniel 3 structural parallel (image + worship command + death decree + faithful refusal) function as the OT type for Rev 13's image and mark? How do the first and second commandments (Exo 20:3-5) establish that worship belongs to God alone? Address the objection that the mark is 'purely spiritual' and not about specific commandments — show that the text itself links the mark to a specific worship system. What is the significance of seal on forehead only (willing allegiance) vs. mark on forehead OR hand (conviction or compliance)?
prior_research_summary: Six prior studies provide substantial foundation: 3am-08-worship-the-creator established the Rev 14:7/Exo 20:11 LXX four-term allusion (poieo/ouranos/ge/thalassa) and the commandment frame (Rev 12:17, 14:12 using entole G1785). 3am-10-babylon-identity established Babylon as the false worship system through zanah/porneia analysis. mark-of-beast-vs-seal-of-god mapped seal (forehead only) vs mark (forehead or hand) and the Deuteronomy 6:6-8 / Ezekiel 9:4 background. rev-13-image-mark-beast-worship-grammar mapped the proskyneo grammatical escalation and the poiei+hina construction binding worship and mark. ot-final-controversy-worship confirmed the Daniel 3 structural parallel with LXX vocabulary links (tselem=eikon, sgid=proskyneo). rev-12-earth-beast-mark established the counterfeit trinity and charagma/charakter etymological connection. This study builds on all of these to establish the worship binary as THE central issue.

required_verses.reason: Proskyneo #1 — spontaneous worship of dragon and beast ('Who is like the beast?')
Proskyneo #2 — universal worship ('all that dwell upon the earth shall worship him')
Proskyneo #3 — coerced worship via earth beast (poiei...hina proskunesousin causative construction)
Image creation + death decree for non-worshippers — Daniel 3 parallel climax
Mark on forehead OR hand + economic coercion — worship enforcement mechanism
Creator-worship alternative — 'worship him that made' echoing Exo 20:11 LXX (poieo)
Third angel's warning — proskyneo of beast/image + mark + wrath without mixture
Saints identified as commandment-keepers with faith of Jesus — the positive identity
OT type: golden image + universal worship command + musical signal + death decree
Faithful refusal of the three Hebrews — paradigm of worship resistance
Death decree executed + divine deliverance — vindicating worship-refusers
First and second commandments — no other gods + no images for worship + proskyneo in LXX
Fourth commandment rationale — Creator formula matching Rev 14:7 (poieo/ouranos/ge/thalassa)
Shema + hand/forehead binding of commandments — OT template for seal/mark location
Seal of God on foreheads — forehead ONLY pattern established
144,000 with Father's name on foreheads — seal content = divine name
Those without seal of God harmed — sealing protects (forehead only)
God's name on foreheads in New Jerusalem — eschatological fulfillment of sealing
Those who refused beast-worship and mark — martyrs vindicated
Tav on foreheads of those who sigh for abominations — OT sealing prototype
Ark of covenant revealed in heaven — commandment context for worship binary
Dragon wars against commandment-keepers — sets up Rev 13's worship coercion
Sabbath as owth (sign) between God and His people — seal vocabulary
Sabbath as sign (owth) — four declarations of Sabbath as sign in Ezekiel
Only OT passage with both sagad (H5456 cognate of Dan 3's sgid) and shachah (H7812)
Creator-worship using ktizo — contrasts with Rev 14:7's poieo (deliberate verb choice)
Sea beast identity — receives dragon's power, seat, authority
Blasphemy against God's name/tabernacle — third commandment counterfeit
Earth beast introduction — lamb-like horns, dragon voice (false prophet)
Christ as charakter of God — theological contrast with charagma (beast's brand)
Satan's temptation: proskyneo me = worship me — Satan's fundamental desire
Sealed with Holy Spirit — critic argument that seal = Spirit, not Sabbath
Sealed unto day of redemption — Spirit as sealing agent
God sealed us and gave Spirit — Spirit as seal agent
Law written in minds (Jer 31:33 fulfillment) — forehead = mind connection
New covenant: law in inward parts, written in hearts — internalization template
Victory over beast/image/mark — worship vindication (song of Moses and Lamb)
Beast and false prophet destroyed — end of false worship system
HOLINESS TO THE LORD on high priest's forehead — inscription inverted by Babylon (Rev 17:5)
MYSTERY BABYLON on harlot's forehead — parody of priestly forehead inscription
Warning against worshipping host of heaven — worship exclusivity
Elijah's worship binary at Carmel: 'How long halt ye between two opinions?'
Joshua's worship binary: 'Choose you this day whom ye will serve'
Everlasting gospel context — first angel introduces worship call
Second angel: Babylon fallen — thumos of her fornication
Fear God + keep commandments + judgment — template paralleling Rev 14:7,12
Cain/Abel — earliest worship binary (accepted vs rejected worship)
First plague on those with mark who worshipped image — worship-mark linkage in judgment
God not to be represented by graven art — NT anti-image principle
Nebuchadnezzar's decree after deliverance — no other god can deliver
Blue ribbon on garment fringes = law-faithfulness color (Beale's priestly parody)
Creator language using ktizo — another contrast with 14:7's poieo

focus_areas.title: The proskyneo verb chain through Revelation 13-14
focus_areas.what: Map every occurrence of proskyneo in Rev 13-14 and trace the grammatical escalation from spontaneous to coerced to death-enforced worship
focus_areas.why: This chain is THE structural backbone of the worship binary — it demonstrates that worship (not the mark, not economics) is the ultimate issue
focus_areas.how: Parse all proskyneo occurrences in Rev 13-14 with greek_parser.py, document tense/mood/voice for each, identify the escalation pattern
focus_areas.narrative: READ the existing word study at `$WORD_STUDIES/G4352-proskyneo.md` and the translation relationship at `$WORD_STUDIES/TR-shachah-proskyneo.md` for the full semantic range and OT-to-NT mapping. Then run `greek_parser.py --verse` on Rev 13:4, 13:8, 13:12, 13:15, 14:7, 14:9, and 14:11 to capture exact morphology of each proskyneo occurrence. The prior study (rev-13-image-mark-beast-worship-grammar) identified a four-stage escalation: spontaneous (13:4, aorist), universal (13:8, future), coerced (13:12, present causative + purpose clause), death-enforced (13:15, conditional + death penalty). Verify these morphological claims and then extend the analysis to 14:7 (the Creator alternative that breaks the chain), 14:9 and 14:11 (present tenses indicating habitual/ongoing worship). The key grammatical point: Rev 14:9,11 use present tenses (proskunei, proskunountes) — why present rather than aorist? What does this say about the habitual nature of the worship choice? Also check proskyneo in Rev 4:10, 5:14, 7:11, 11:1, 11:16, 15:4, 19:4, 19:10, 22:8-9 to establish the broader Revelation worship pattern. Confirm the statistic that proskyneo occurs 24 times in Revelation (40% of NT total). Run `cross_testament_parallels_v2.py --hybrid-nt` on Rev 13:4 and Rev 14:7.

focus_areas.title: The poiei + hina construction binding worship and mark
focus_areas.what: Analyze the grammatical construction that binds worship coercion to mark imposition in Rev 13:12-17
focus_areas.why: The grammar itself proves that worship is the ultimate purpose and the mark is the instrument — answering 'is the mark about worship or something else?'
focus_areas.how: Parse Rev 13:12, 13:15-16 with greek_parser.py; identify identical poiei...hina patterns; trace the purpose-clause hierarchy
focus_areas.narrative: The prior study identified that Rev 13:12 (poiei...hina proskunesousin) and Rev 13:16 (poiei...hina dosin...charagma) use identical grammatical structures. Run `greek_parser.py --verse` on Rev 13:12 and Rev 13:16 to verify this claim. READ the grammar reference at `$GRAMMAR_REF/greek/purpose-clause-hina-aorist-subjunctive.md` and `$GRAMMAR_REF/greek/aorist-infinitive-accusative-of-duration.md` (Rev 13:5 poiesai) for context. The purpose-clause hierarchy establishes: worship = ULTIMATE PURPOSE (v.12), mark = INSTRUMENT (v.16), economic control = MECHANISM (v.17). Also check the genitive shift noted in the prior study: dative in v.14 ('image FOR the beast') to genitive in v.15 ('image OF the beast'). Run `greek_parser.py --verse` on Rev 13:14 and Rev 13:15 to verify this grammatical shift. Key question: does every passage outside Rev 13:16-17 that mentions charagma also mention proskyneo and/or eikon? Check Rev 14:9, 14:11, 16:2, 19:20, 20:4 to verify that the grammar NEVER presents the mark in isolation from worship.

focus_areas.title: The Daniel 3 / Revelation 13 structural parallel
focus_areas.what: Verify the five-element structural parallel and specific LXX vocabulary links between Daniel 3 and Revelation 13
focus_areas.why: If Daniel 3 is the OT type for Revelation 13, it establishes that the final crisis follows the pattern of image + universal worship command + death decree + faithful refusal + divine vindication
focus_areas.how: Compare Dan 3 LXX vocabulary with Rev 13 Greek; verify tselem/eikon, sgid/proskyneo, and peoples/nations/tongues parallels
focus_areas.narrative: READ the Aramaic word studies at `$WORD_STUDIES/A6755-tselem.md` (Aramaic tselem = image) and `$WORD_STUDIES/A5457-cgid.md` (Aramaic sgid = prostrate worship) plus the Hebrew/Greek counterparts `$WORD_STUDIES/H6754-tselem.md` and `$WORD_STUDIES/G1504-eikon.md`. The prior study (ot-final-controversy-worship) confirmed: tselem maps to eikon through LXX, sgid maps to proskyneo through LXX, and the peoples/nations/tongues formula in Dan 3:4 maps to Rev 13:7. Verify by running `hebrew_parser.py --verse` on Dan 3:1, 3:5, 3:6, 3:14-15 and `greek_parser.py --verse` on Rev 13:14-15. The five elements to verify: (1) image created (Dan 3:1 / Rev 13:14), (2) universal worship commanded (Dan 3:4-5 / Rev 13:15), (3) death decreed for refusal (Dan 3:6 / Rev 13:15), (4) faithful remnant refuses (Dan 3:16-18 / Rev 14:12), (5) God vindicates (Dan 3:25-28 / Rev 15:2). Also verify Paulien's 666/60x6 numerical connection: Dan 3:1 image is 60 cubits tall × 6 cubits wide; Rev 13:18 gives 666. Run `cross_testament_parallels_v2.py --hybrid-ot` on Dan 3:5-6 to check for Revelation connections.

focus_areas.title: The first and second commandments as worship-exclusivity foundation
focus_areas.what: Establish from Exodus 20:3-6 (via LXX proskyneo) that worship belongs to God alone and that Revelation's proskyneo chain deliberately echoes the Sinai prohibition
focus_areas.why: If the LXX uses proskyneo in the commandment prohibition, then Revelation's entire proskyneo chain is an echo of Sinai — connecting the end-time crisis to the Decalogue
focus_areas.how: Check LXX text of Exo 20:3-6 for proskyneo; compare with Deut 5:7-10 LXX; trace the vocabulary chain into Revelation
focus_areas.narrative: READ `$WORD_STUDIES/H7812-shachah.md` and `$WORD_STUDIES/TR-shachah-proskyneo.md` to understand the Hebrew-to-Greek mapping. The critical question: does the LXX of Exodus 20:5 use proskyneo to translate shachah in 'thou shalt not bow down to them'? If yes, then every time John uses proskyneo in Revelation's worship chain, he is echoing the Sinai commandment. Run `greek_parser.py --verse` on the LXX text of Exo 20:3-5 if possible, or use the translation relationship data to confirm the mapping. Also check Deut 5:9 for the same. READ `$WORD_STUDIES/G1785-entole.md` to understand how commandments function in Revelation (Rev 12:17, 14:12). The cmd-03-second-commandment-no-images prior study noted that the three-domain formula (heaven above, earth beneath, water under the earth) in Exo 20:4 recurs in Rev 14:7. Verify this connection. Also trace the verb shachah through Isa 44:15-17 where it co-occurs with sagad (H5456, cognate of Daniel 3's Aramaic sgid) — this is the only OT passage with both worship verbs.

focus_areas.title: Seal on forehead only vs. mark on forehead OR hand
focus_areas.what: Verify that every seal passage is forehead-only and every mark passage is forehead-or-hand, and establish the theological significance of this distinction
focus_areas.why: The asymmetry proves that God requires genuine heart allegiance (forehead = mind/conviction) while the beast accepts mere external compliance (hand = action without conviction)
focus_areas.how: Check all seal/name-on-forehead passages; check all mark passages; verify Deut 6:8 and Ezek 9:4 as OT backgrounds
focus_areas.narrative: READ `$WORD_STUDIES/G4973-sphragis.md` (seal), `$WORD_STUDIES/G5480-charagma.md` (mark), and `$WORD_STUDIES/G3359-metopon.md` (forehead) for the lexical foundations. The key pattern to verify: God's seal/name appears on forehead ONLY in Rev 7:3, 9:4, 14:1, 22:4 — never on the hand. The beast's mark appears on forehead OR hand in Rev 13:16, 14:9, 14:11, 20:4 — the disjunction is critical. Run `greek_parser.py --verse` on Rev 7:3, Rev 13:16, and Rev 14:9 to confirm the exact Greek construction. The Deuteronomy 6:8 background ('bind them for a sign upon thine hand, and they shall be as frontlets between thine eyes') establishes the OT template that the beast counterfeits. READ `$WORD_STUDIES/H226-owth.md` and `$WORD_STUDIES/TR-owth-semeion.md` for the sign/seal vocabulary chain. Ezekiel 9:4 (tav on foreheads of those who sigh) is the direct OT prototype for Rev 7:3. Also check Exo 28:36-38 — the high priest's forehead inscription 'HOLINESS TO THE LORD' that Babylon parodies in Rev 17:5. The charagma/charakter etymological connection (shared root charasso) is theologically significant: Christ bears God's charakter (Heb 1:3); beast-followers bear the beast's charagma. READ `$WORD_STUDIES/G5481-charakter.md` for this connection.

focus_areas.title: The Rev 14:7 / Exo 20:11 LXX allusion and the Sabbath-Creator link
focus_areas.what: Verify Paulien's claim that Rev 14:7 uses four identical terms from Exo 20:11 LXX in the same order, establishing the Creator-worship call as a fourth-commandment allusion
focus_areas.why: If the most certain OT allusion in Revelation links the Creator-worship call to the Sabbath commandment, the worship binary has a specific commandment content — directly countering the 'purely spiritual' objection
focus_areas.how: Compare Greek text of Rev 14:7 and Exo 20:11 LXX term by term; check why poieo is used instead of ktizo
focus_areas.narrative: READ `$WORD_STUDIES/G4160-poieo.md` for the verb choice analysis. The prior study (3am-08) established that Rev 14:7 uses poieo (made) while every other creation text in Revelation uses ktizo (Rev 4:11, 10:6). This deliberate verb choice maps Rev 14:7 to Exo 20:11 LXX where poieo translates asah (H6213). READ `$WORD_STUDIES/H6213-asah.md` for the Hebrew side. The four terms to verify in sequence: poieo (made), ouranos (heaven), ge (earth), thalassa (sea). READ `$WORD_STUDIES/G3772-ouranos.md`, `$WORD_STUDIES/G1093-ge.md`, `$WORD_STUDIES/G2281-thalassa.md` for the creation-domain vocabulary. Run `greek_parser.py --verse` on Rev 14:7 and compare with Exo 20:11 LXX text. Also compare with Rev 4:11 and Rev 10:6 to show the ktizo/poieo contrast. Note the fourth element: Rev 14:7 adds 'fountains of waters' (pegas hydaton) — READ `$WORD_STUDIES/G4077-pege.md` to check whether this maps to anything in Exo 20:11. The Ecclesiastes 12:13-14 template (fear God + commandments + judgment) parallels Rev 14:7,12 — verify this structural echo.

focus_areas.title: Answering the 'purely spiritual' objection
focus_areas.what: Demonstrate that even scholars who call the mark 'symbolic' link it to a worship system, and that the text itself connects the worship binary to specific commandments
focus_areas.why: The strongest counter-argument to the worship-binary thesis claims the mark is purely spiritual with no commandment content — this must be addressed textually
focus_areas.how: Trace the textual evidence: Rev 14:7 echoes Exo 20:11, Rev 14:12 identifies commandment-keepers, Rev 11:19 reveals the ark of the covenant, and Beale himself acknowledges the seal/mark parody structure
focus_areas.narrative: This focus area synthesizes findings from other areas into an argument against the 'purely spiritual' objection. The evidence chain to build: (1) Rev 14:7's Creator-worship call alludes to the fourth commandment (Exo 20:11 LXX — verified in focus area 6). (2) Rev 14:12 identifies the saints as those who keep 'the commandments of God' (tas entolas tou theou) — run `greek_parser.py --verse` on Rev 14:12 and check entole (READ `$WORD_STUDIES/G1785-entole.md`). (3) Rev 11:19 reveals the ark of the covenant (containing the commandments) immediately before the Rev 12-14 worship conflict. (4) Rev 12:17 identifies the dragon's target as commandment-keepers. (5) Even Beale (Reformed, NIGTC) calls the mark 'a twisted parody of the real thing' and acknowledges Deut 6 background. (6) Kroll (GCI) explicitly says 'their worship IS the mark' — conceding the worship-system connection while denying specific commandment content. (7) The Answering Adventism critic claims seal = Holy Spirit (Eph 1:13, 4:30) — but the Adventist claim is that Spirit is the sealing AGENT while the Sabbath is the SIGN of allegiance. Verify from the text whether these are compatible (the prior response study already addressed this). The research agent should retrieve Eph 1:13, 4:30, 2 Cor 1:22 alongside Rev 7:2-3 and 14:1.

focus_areas.title: Beast activities as counterfeit of the first four commandments
focus_areas.what: Verify Bohr/Paulien's claim that the beast's activities in Rev 13 systematically counterfeit each of the first four commandments
focus_areas.why: If the beast's program maps to a systematic counterfeiting of the first table of the Decalogue, the worship binary is inherently about commandments
focus_areas.how: Map each beast activity to each commandment: exclusive worship (1st), image worship (2nd), blasphemy of name (3rd), counterfeit sign/mark (4th)
focus_areas.narrative: The prior study (3am-08) noted Paulien's structural argument: Rev 13's beast activities systematically counterfeit the First Table of the Decalogue. Verify by retrieving full text of Rev 13:4-8 (first commandment counterfeit: beast demands exclusive worship), Rev 13:14-15 (second commandment counterfeit: image to worship), Rev 13:5-6 (third commandment counterfeit: blasphemes God's name), Rev 13:16-17 (fourth commandment counterfeit: mark/seal as sign of allegiance). Run `greek_parser.py --verse` on Rev 13:6 to check the blasphemy language. READ `$WORD_STUDIES/G2342-therion.md` for the beast vocabulary. Also check the 'no rest day nor night' in Rev 14:11 (ouk echousin anapausin) — READ `$WORD_STUDIES/G372-anapausis.md` for the rest/Sabbath irony connection. The prior study noted this stands in contrast to the sabbatismos remaining for God's people (Heb 4:9). Retrieve Heb 4:9 and check whether the anapausis/sabbatismos vocabulary connects Rev 14:11 to the Sabbath rest theme.

focus_areas.title: The escalation pattern from individual to global worship tests in Scripture
focus_areas.what: Trace the escalation of worship tests from individual (Cain/Abel) to national (golden calf, Carmel) to international (Daniel 3) to global-eschatological (Rev 13-14)
focus_areas.why: The pattern demonstrates that the Rev 13-14 worship binary is the climax of a consistent biblical trajectory, not an isolated apocalyptic image
focus_areas.how: Map worship-test narratives across Scripture; show how each escalates in scope and stakes
focus_areas.narrative: The prior study (ot-final-controversy-worship) identified the escalation pattern: individual (Cain/Abel, Gen 4:3-7), priestly (Nadab/Abihu, Lev 10:1-2), national (golden calf, Exo 32; Carmel, 1 Kings 18), international (Daniel 3), global-eschatological (Rev 13-14). Retrieve Gen 4:3-7, Lev 10:1-2, Exo 32:1-8, 1 Kings 18:21-39, Dan 3:1-7, and Rev 13:4-8. The key insight: at each stage the scope widens, the coercion intensifies, and the stakes increase. The Elijah/Carmel test is particularly relevant because it presents an explicit worship binary: 'How long halt ye between two opinions? if the LORD be God, follow him: but if Baal, then follow him' (1 Kings 18:21). This is the OT expression of the same binary that Rev 13-14 presents on a global scale. Also include Joshua 24:15 ('choose you this day whom ye will serve') as an explicit worship-choice text. Run `cross_testament_parallels_v2.py --hybrid-ot` on 1 Kings 18:21 to check for Revelation connections.

focus_areas.title: Charagma vs. sphragis: the lexical theology of mark vs. seal
focus_areas.what: Compare the full semantic range of charagma (mark/brand) and sphragis (seal/signet) to establish their contrasting theological implications
focus_areas.why: The lexical distinction reinforces the worship binary: God seals (authorizes, protects, values) while the beast brands (controls, marks, commodifies)
focus_areas.how: Read library entries for both terms; trace all NT occurrences; compare the ownership/authority implications
focus_areas.narrative: READ `$WORD_STUDIES/G5480-charagma.md` and `$WORD_STUDIES/G4973-sphragis.md` for the canonical studies. Charagma occurs 8 times in the NT — check whether all 8 are in Revelation (7 for the beast's mark + 1 in Acts 17:29 for graven art). Sphragis occurs in multiple NT contexts — check the distribution between Pauline (Eph 1:13, 4:30, 2 Cor 1:22, Rom 4:11) and Revelation (Rev 5:1-9, 6:1-12, 7:2, 8:1, 9:4). The shared root charasso connects charagma and charakter (G5481) — READ `$WORD_STUDIES/G5481-charakter.md` for the Heb 1:3 connection. The theological irony: Christ bears God's character (charakter, Heb 1:3); beast-followers bear the beast's brand (charagma). Also check the Hebrew background: chatham (H2856, to seal) — READ `$WORD_STUDIES/H2856-chatham.md` — and tav (H8420, mark) in Ezek 9:4. The web research noted that charagma implies 'external, often imposed control' while sphragis emphasizes 'divine authorization and protective assurance.' Verify this lexical contrast from the word study data.

required_word_studies.reason: THE central word — worship verb chain through Rev 13-14
Mark of the beast — 8 NT occurrences, lexical analysis needed
Seal of God — contrast with charagma
Image of the beast — maps to tselem in Daniel 3
Creator verb in Rev 14:7 matching Exo 20:11 — deliberate choice over ktizo
Character of God (Heb 1:3) — shared root with charagma
Forehead — appears in both seal and mark passages
Hebrew worship verb mapping to proskyneo through LXX
Beast — central to the worship binary
Rest — 'no rest day nor night' (Rev 14:11) vs. sabbatismos
Wrath — God's orge in the third angel's warning
Aramaic prostrate worship in Daniel 3 — maps to proskyneo
Aramaic image in Daniel 3 — maps to eikon
Hebrew image — imago Dei and idol image connections
Service-worship — another worship term in Revelation context
Hebrew cognate of Aramaic sgid — co-occurs with shachah in Isa 44:15-17

required_parsing.reason: Proskyneo #2 — future tense of universal worship
Death decree conditional — image given breath/speech
Mark imposition — poiei + hina construction parallel to v.12
Creator-worship call — poieo verb form and domain terms
Warning proskyneo — present tense indicating habitual worship
No rest + ongoing worship — present participle proskunountes
Seal on foreheads — verify forehead-only pattern
LXX proskyneo in commandment prohibition — verify shachah mapping
Aramaic worship command + death decree vocabulary

required_parallels.reason: OT and NT parallels for spontaneous beast-worship
OT allusion to Exo 20:11 and creation texts
Revelation connections to Daniel 3 worship command

word_studies.description: charagma (mark/brand), G5480 — mark of the beast
eikon (image), G1504 — image of the beast / image of God
poieo (make/do), G4160 — Creator verb in Rev 14:7
charakter (express image), G5481 — Heb 1:3 / charagma contrast
shachah (worship/bow down), H7812 — Hebrew worship verb
orge (wrath), G3709 — divine wrath in third angel
tselem (Hebrew image), H6754 — image vocabulary chain
arnion (lamb), G721 — Lamb worship in Revelation
didomi (give), G1325 — authority given to beast

grammar_references.description: hina + aorist subjunctive purpose clause — Rev 13:12,16 poiei...hina construction
Negative purpose clause — relevant to worship prohibition
Aorist passive — Rev 13:3,5,7 edothe (was given) authorization pattern
poieo + acc. of time — Rev 13:5 poiesai menas...dyo
Prophetic aorist — Rev 14:8 Babylon is fallen (epesen)
Divine passive — edothe pattern marking sovereign permission
Present active indicative — Rev 14:9,11 habitual worship tenses
Apocalyptic visionary recursion — Rev 12-14 structural flow
Perfect passive participle — Rev 13:3 esphagmenen (slain state)
Vision introduction formulas — kai eidon framing in Rev 13-14
Present participle legon — Rev 14 parallel angel structure
Future passive indicative — Rev 14:10 basanisthesetai (shall be tormented)
Third-class conditional — Matt 4:9 ean...proskyneses (Satan's worship temptation)

missing_library_entries.reason: Hebrew cognate of Aramaic sgid — only appears 4x in OT, co-occurs with shachah in Isa 44:15-17; critical for worship vocabulary chain
Hebrew forehead — Ezek 9:4, Exo 28:38 background for metopon in Revelation
Frontlets between eyes (Deut 6:8, Exo 13:16) — OT forehead binding template
Create — the verb NOT used in Rev 14:7 (poieo chosen instead); 4:11, 10:6 contrast
Spirit as sealing agent (Eph 1:13, 4:30) — needed for critic engagement
Grammar entry exists but needs specific application to Rev 13:12,16 poiei+hina pattern

topics_discovered.name: WORSHIP
topics_discovered.subtopic: To be rendered only to God

topics_discovered.name: PERSECUTION
topics_discovered.subtopic: Of the three Hebrew young men

external_corpus_leads.claim: The third angel's message specifically warns against beast/image worship and mark-reception, contrasting with those who keep God's commandments
external_corpus_leads.directive: Verify directly from Rev 14:9-12 — does the text itself create this contrast between beast-worshippers and commandment-keepers?

external_corpus_leads.claim: Daniel 3 and Revelation 13 share the same principles: universal worship mandate, death decree, faithful refusal
external_corpus_leads.directive: Verify by comparing the five structural elements and LXX vocabulary connections between Dan 3 and Rev 13

external_corpus_leads.claim: Forehead = mind/judgment; hand = outward compliance; God requires forehead only, beast accepts hand
external_corpus_leads.directive: Verify from the seal passages (Rev 7:3, 14:1, 22:4 — forehead only) vs mark passages (Rev 13:16, 14:9, 20:4 — forehead OR hand)

external_corpus_leads.claim: Sealing in the forehead = writing in the mind (Heb 10:16 / Jer 31:33)
external_corpus_leads.directive: Verify the Heb 10:16 / Jer 31:33 connection — does the new covenant 'law in minds' concept link to forehead sealing?

external_corpus_leads.claim: Daniel 3 is the 'foundational background' to Rev 13:11-18
external_corpus_leads.directive: Verify by comparing structural elements and vocabulary connections through the LXX

external_corpus_leads.claim: Beast's activities map to the first four commandments: worship (1st), image (2nd), blasphemy (3rd), counterfeit sign (4th)
external_corpus_leads.directive: Verify against Rev 13:4-6 (worship + blasphemy) and Rev 13:14-17 (image + mark) — does each activity correspond to a commandment?

external_corpus_leads.claim: The seal/mark opposition requires the mark to be 'a day of worship that rivals the Sabbath'
external_corpus_leads.directive: Test whether the text itself identifies the mark with a specific practice or leaves it symbolic — trace the evidence chain from Rev 14:7 (Exo 20:11 allusion) through 14:12 (commandment-keepers)

external_corpus_leads.claim: Worship vs. idolatry is THE central theme of Revelation — 'There are clearly only two options'
external_corpus_leads.directive: Verify by counting proskyneo occurrences in Revelation (claimed 24/60 NT total = 40%) and mapping the worship-binary structure

external_corpus_leads.claim: Rev 14:7 uses 'four identical terms in the same order' as Exo 20:11 — 'the most certain OT allusion in Revelation'
external_corpus_leads.directive: Verify term by term: compare Greek of Rev 14:7 with Exo 20:11 LXX — poieo, ouranos, ge, thalassa

external_corpus_leads.claim: The mark is 'primarily symbolic' but 'a twisted parody of the real thing' — God's seal parodied by beast's mark
external_corpus_leads.directive: Verify the parody structure by comparing seal language (Rev 7:2-3) with mark language (Rev 13:16-17)

external_corpus_leads.claim: The seal of God is the Holy Spirit (Eph 1:13, 4:30), not the Sabbath
external_corpus_leads.directive: Check Eph 1:13, 4:30, 2 Cor 1:22 (seal = Spirit) against Rev 7:2-3 and 14:1 (seal = name) — are these the same sealing or compatible sealings?

external_corpus_leads.claim: John's original audience needed immediate application, so the mark cannot be about future events
external_corpus_leads.directive: Assess whether apocalyptic genre requires immediate fulfillment — check prophetic literature genre conventions

external_corpus_leads.claim: 'Their worship IS the mark' — worship determines identity, not a visible sign
external_corpus_leads.directive: Note that even this non-Adventist source concedes the worship-system connection — use as evidence that the 'purely spiritual' objection still links mark to worship

external_corpus_leads.claim: The Deut 6 hand/forehead command makes the beast's mark 'a counterfeit of the mark of God'
external_corpus_leads.directive: Verify the Deut 6:8 connection — does the hand/forehead language of the mark echo the commandment-binding language?

external_corpus_leads.claim: Charagma refers to imperial stamps on purchase contracts — 666 = Nero Caesar gematria
external_corpus_leads.directive: Present as historical context but assess whether preterist reading accounts for the full Rev 13-14 worship structure

research_instructions:
## Critical Research Directives

1. **Proskyneo statistics verification**: Confirm that proskyneo occurs exactly 24 times in Revelation and map every single occurrence with verse reference and morphological data. This is a factual claim from web research that must be verified.

2. **LXX verification for Exo 20:5**: The single most important lexical question in this study is whether the Septuagint uses proskyneo to translate shachah in the commandment prohibition. If confirmed, the entire Revelation proskyneo chain echoes Sinai.

3. **Charagma isolation test**: Check every NT occurrence of charagma (claimed 8 total) and verify whether it EVER appears without proskyneo or eikon in the same context. If the mark is never grammatically separated from worship, the 'purely spiritual' objection has no textual ground.

4. **Verb choice diagnostic**: Rev 14:7 uses poieo while Rev 4:11 and 10:6 use ktizo for creation. The research agent should run greek_parser.py on all three to confirm the lexical difference and assess whether this constitutes a deliberate allusion to Exo 20:11.

5. **Forehead-only pattern exhaustive check**: Every seal/name-on-forehead passage must be checked to confirm the pattern holds with NO exceptions. Any exception would weaken the argument significantly.

6. **Dan 3 LXX text retrieval**: If possible, retrieve the LXX text of Daniel 3:1-7 to verify that tselem maps to eikon and the peoples/nations/tongues formula uses the same vocabulary as Rev 13:7.

7. **READ all available library entries listed above BEFORE running tool commands** — the canonical word studies contain verified occurrence counts, translation distributions, and semantic ranges that save duplication.

8. **Sabbath sign passages**: Retrieve Exo 31:13, 31:17, Ezek 20:12, and Ezek 20:20 to verify that the Sabbath is explicitly called owth (sign) four times. No other commandment receives this designation — verify by checking whether any other commandment is called owth.
